def: A disease of horses and cattle accompanied by carbuncles in themouth and on the tongue.
To form an abscess; to swell and fill with pus. Wiseman.

Expressing the type, structure, relations, and reactions of acompound; graphic; -- said of formulæ. See under Formula. Rationalhorizon. (Astron.) See Horizon, 2 (b).-- Rational quantity (Alg. ), one that can be expressed without theuse of a radical sign, or in extract parts of unity; -- opposed toirrational or radical quantity.-- Rational symptom (Med.), one elicited by the statements of thepatient himself and not as the result of a physical examination.
Short and rapid breathing; a panting; asthma. Glanvill.

Two;- nearly obsolete in common discourse, but used in poetryand burlesque. "Children twain." Chaucer.And whosoever shall compel thee to go a mile, go with him twain.Matt. v. 41.In twain, in halves; into two parts; asunder.When old winder split the rocks in twain. Dryden.-- Twain cloud. (Meteor.) Same as Cumulo-stratus.
A compound of hydrobromic acid with a base; -- distinguishedfrom a bromide, in which only the bromine unites with the base.
A Linnæan class of plants, whose stamens and pistils are indistinct flowers in the same plant.
To be dull and spiritless. "Moping melancholy." Milton.A sickly part of one true sense Could not so mope. Shak.
A house for children to play in or to play with; a playhouse.
